questions.question: A music producer mixes $2$ parts of a fast rhythm pattern with $3$ parts of a slow rhythm pattern. What is the ratio of fast to total rhythm patterns?
A painter mixes a $15\%$ red paint solution with a $30\%$ red paint solution to obtain $12$ ounces of a $21\%$ red paint solution. How many ounces of the $15\%$ solution are used?
A DJ mixes $150$ songs with a rhythm frequency of $120$ beats per minute (bpm) with $50$ songs with a rhythm frequency of $100$ bpm. What is the average rhythm frequency of the mixed songs?
A musician records a $3$-minute song with a $1:2$ ratio of fast to slow notes. If the song has $180$ notes, how many fast notes are there?
An artist paints a $4:5$ ratio of red to blue pixels on a $20\times30$ canvas. How many blue pixels are there?
A composer writes a $5$-minute melody with a $2:3$ ratio of high to low notes. If the melody has $240$ notes, how many low notes are there?
A band mixes a $25\%$ instrumental song with a $50\%$ instrumental song to obtain a $30\%$ instrumental song. What is the ratio of the first song to the second song?
A painter mixes $2$ gallons of a $30\%$ blue paint solution with $1$ gallon of a $50\%$ blue paint solution. What percentage of the mixture is blue?

A music producer mixes two types of sound effects, A and B, with the following characteristics: A has a $20\%$ chance of being a $100$-decibel sound and an $80\%$ chance of being a $50$-decibel sound. B has a $30\%$ chance of being a $120$-decibel sound and a $70\%$ chance of being a $40$-decibel sound. If the producer mixes $3$ parts of sound effect A with $2$ parts of sound effect B, what is the probability that the resulting sound effect will be a $100$-decibel sound?

An artist paints a $12\times18$ canvas with a $3:5$ ratio of blue to red pixels. If the artist then adds a $2\times3$ section with a $2:3$ ratio of blue to red pixels, what is the new ratio of blue to total pixels on the canvas?

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use equations to solve mixture problems
Tip 2: Remind students to interpret concentration and ratios in the context of arts and music
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of concise and unique problem-solving
